content.content:
Web search results for query: "Nexus Mods redesign 2024 card grid layout UX tile view CSS implementation"

Links: [{"title":"An Update on the Mod Browsing Overhaul at Nexus mods and community","url":"https://www.nexusmods.com/news/15239"},{"title":"New Nexus Mods design launched at Nexus mods and community","url":"https://www.nexusmods.com/news/13338"},{"title":"Phone - Overhaul at TCG Card Shop Simulator Nexus - Mods and community","url":"https://www.nexusmods.com/tcgcardshopsimulator/mods/685"},{"title":"An Update on the Mod Browsing Overhaul at Lunar Remastered Collection Nexus - Mods and community","url":"https://www.nexusmods.com/lunarremasteredcollection/news/15239"},{"title":"Where are we at with the redesign? at Myth of Empires Nexus - Mods and community","url":"https://www.nexusmods.com/mythofempires/news/12620"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Nexus Mods redesign and its grid layout:

The redesign includes grid and list view options as display formats for browsing mods, which many users preferred for offering more content visibility on the page.

The new design improves the hierarchy of content, making it easier to browse across different sections on a page while standardizing elements of the layout across different pages. However, some of these changes mean adding in extra space to improve the hierarchy - the trade-off is that some extra scrolling is required.

Regarding the design philosophy, Nexus is focused on seeking designs that load faster and standardize the design.

Unfortunately, the search results don't contain specific technical details about CSS implementation or the tile view structure for the card grid layout. The results focus more on the UX philosophy and user feedback regarding the layout changes rather than the underlying technical implementation details.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
